questions.q: The Speaker is the presiding officer of which House of the Indian Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Speaker is the presiding officer of the Lok Sabha, the lower house of Parliament.

questions.q: Who was the first Speaker of independent India's Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Ganesh Vasudev Mavalankar was the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: How is the Speaker of the Lok Sabha chosen?
options: Elected by members of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Speaker is elected by the members of the Lok Sabha from among themselves.

questions.q: One main duty of the Speaker is to maintain what during sittings of the House?
questions.explanation: The Speaker conducts the business of the House and maintains order and discipline.

questions.q: The Speaker must be a member of which body?
questions.explanation: The Speaker is elected from among the members of the Lok Sabha and is therefore one of its members.

questions.q: Who presides over the Lok Sabha in the absence of the Speaker?
questions.explanation: In the Speaker's absence, the Deputy Speaker presides over the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The Speaker certifies whether a bill is a Money Bill, a power exercised under which type of provision?
questions.explanation: The Constitution gives the Speaker the power to certify whether a bill is a Money Bill.

questions.q: In which year did G.V. Mavalankar become the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: G.V. Mavalankar became the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha in 1952.

questions.q: The ceremonial mace of the House is placed near whose chair when the Lok Sabha is in session?
questions.explanation: The ceremonial mace is placed near the Speaker's chair to symbolise the authority of the presiding officer.

questions.q: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha is often described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Speaker is the presiding officer responsible for conducting the business of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Articles 93 and 94 of the Constitution deal primarily with which office?
options: The Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Articles 93 and 94 provide for the offices of the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha and their term.

questions.q: When does the Speaker of the Lok Sabha normally cast a vote?
options: Only in the case of a tie (a casting vote)
questions.explanation: The Speaker does not vote in the first instance but exercises a casting vote in the case of an equality of votes.

questions.q: Under the Tenth Schedule, who decides on the disqualification of a Lok Sabha member on grounds of defection?
questions.explanation: The Speaker is the deciding authority on questions of disqualification under the anti-defection provisions of the Tenth Schedule.

questions.q: To whom does the Speaker of the Lok Sabha submit a resignation letter?
questions.explanation: The Speaker may resign by writing addressed to the Deputy Speaker.

questions.q: When the Lok Sabha is dissolved, the Speaker continues in office until when?
options: Until just before the first meeting of the newly elected Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Speaker does not vacate office on dissolution and continues until immediately before the first meeting of the next Lok Sabha.

questions.q: A resolution to remove the Speaker from office requires what kind of notice?
options: At least 14 days notice of intention to move the resolution
questions.explanation: A resolution to remove the Speaker can be moved only after at least 14 days notice of the intention to move it.

questions.q: Who presided over the Lok Sabha as the first Speaker of independent India before the first general elections, in the provisional Parliament?
questions.explanation: G.V. Mavalankar served as Speaker of the provisional Parliament and then continued as the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Which committee of the Lok Sabha is chaired ex-officio by the Speaker?
questions.explanation: The Speaker is the ex-officio chairperson of the Business Advisory Committee of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: G.V. Mavalankar is often given which honorary title for shaping the early procedures of the House?
questions.explanation: G.V. Mavalankar is often called the Father of the Lok Sabha for shaping its early procedures.

questions.q: The salaries and allowances of the Speaker are charged on which fund, making them non-votable?
questions.explanation: The Speaker's salary and allowances are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India and are not subject to the vote of Parliament.

questions.q: Who acted as the protem (temporary) presiding officer customarily appointed before a new Speaker is elected?
options: The senior-most member, as the pro tem Speaker
questions.explanation: A pro tem Speaker, customarily a senior member, presides over the initial sittings until a new Speaker is elected.

questions.q: The Speaker presides over a joint sitting of both Houses of Parliament under which scenario?
options: Whenever the Rajya Sabha requests it
When a joint sitting is summoned to resolve a deadlock on a bill
questions.explanation: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha presides over a joint sitting of both Houses convened to resolve a deadlock over a bill.

questions.q: G.V. Mavalankar, the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha, served in that office until which year?
questions.explanation: G.V. Mavalankar served as Speaker of the Lok Sabha from 1952 until his death in 1956.

questions.q: Who succeeded G.V. Mavalankar to become the second Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: M. Ananthasayanam Ayyangar became the second Speaker of the Lok Sabha after G.V. Mavalankar.

questions.q: Which former Speaker of the Lok Sabha later went on to become President of India?
questions.explanation: Neelam Sanjiva Reddy served as Speaker of the Lok Sabha and later became President of India.

questions.q: Sumitra Mahajan, who served as Speaker from 2014 to 2019, was the second woman to hold the office after which Speaker?
questions.explanation: Meira Kumar was the first woman Speaker of the Lok Sabha, and Sumitra Mahajan was the second.

questions.q: Who was the first woman to be elected Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Meira Kumar, elected in 2009, was the first woman Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: A decision of the Speaker certifying a bill as a Money Bill is, by the text of the Constitution, declared to be what?
options: Open to a Rajya Sabha veto
questions.explanation: The Constitution states that the Speaker's certificate that a bill is a Money Bill is final.

questions.q: The office of Deputy Speaker, who assists the Speaker, is provided for under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 93 provides for the election of both the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: While a resolution for the Speaker's removal is under consideration, the Speaker may speak in and take part in the proceedings but cannot do what?
options: Cast a normal vote, though may vote in the first instance only on that resolution
questions.explanation: While a removal resolution is under consideration, the Speaker may take part but votes only in the first instance and not in the case of a tie on that resolution.
